methodology: Each human memory system is scored covered (1.0), partial (0.5), or planned (0.0). The overall coverage percentage is the weight-normalized sum of status scores across all systems, where weight reflects each system's relative importance to an always-on agent memory. This file is the single source of truth, mirrored from src/cognifold/brain/memory_coverage.json.

systems.name: Working Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Hierarchical context window (immediate / working / background) surfaces the most relevant nodes per event via composite scoring.
systems.description: Transient, capacity-limited store that holds and manipulates information for the task at hand. CogniFold realizes it as a budgeted, three-level context window assembled fresh for every event.

systems.name: Episodic Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: EVENT nodes capture what-happened-when verbatim; entity-anchored boosting and raw-turn preservation keep episodes retrievable.
systems.description: Memory for specific autobiographical events bound to a time and place. The Hippocampal EVENT layer is CogniFold's verbatim episodic substrate.

systems.name: Semantic Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: CONCEPT nodes emerge from repeated EVENTs; the agent folds episodes into generalized, grounded knowledge with explainable reasoning.
systems.description: General world knowledge and facts abstracted away from any single experience. The Neocortical CONCEPT layer crystallizes semantics from the event stream.

systems.name: Prospective Memory (Intent)
systems.cognifold_mechanism: INTENT nodes crystallize proactively from accumulated concepts and time anchors — remembering to act in the future is a property of the topology, not the agent policy.
systems.description: Remembering to carry out intended actions at the right future moment. This proactive intent crystallization is CogniFold's unique differentiator versus passive memory stores.

systems.name: Temporal Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: TIME nodes anchor deadlines and schedules; recency decay and urgency boosting make approaching deadlines salient in retrieval.
systems.description: Encoding of when events occurred and their order, enabling deadline awareness and time-sensitive reasoning. TIME nodes plus recency/urgency scoring provide CogniFold's temporal axis.

systems.name: Memory Consolidation
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Inter-session consolidation merges similar concepts and reinforces grounded structure; offline systems-level replay is on the roadmap.
systems.description: The gradual transfer of memories from fast hippocampal storage to durable neocortical representations. CogniFold consolidates at session boundaries; richer sleep-like offline replay is planned.

systems.name: Adaptive Forgetting
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Recency decay lowers stale node salience and orphan pruning removes unsupported concepts; principled importance-weighted decay is in progress.
systems.description: Active down-weighting and removal of low-value memories to keep the store relevant. CogniFold decays and prunes today; calibrated, value-aware forgetting is the next step.

systems.name: Procedural Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Planned: skills and learned action sequences captured as reusable procedure nodes distinct from declarative concepts.
systems.description: Implicit memory for skills and habits — how to do things. Not yet modeled; a procedure layer is on the roadmap.

systems.name: Priming
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Planned: residual activation that biases retrieval toward recently or repeatedly encountered patterns.
systems.description: Implicit facilitation where exposure to a stimulus speeds later processing of related stimuli. A retrieval-bias mechanism is planned.

systems.name: Associative Conditioning
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Planned: reinforcement-driven edge strengthening between co-occurring stimuli and outcomes.
systems.description: Learned associations between stimuli and responses through repeated pairing. Reinforcement-weighted edges are a planned extension.

systems.name: Affective Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Planned: emotional salience tags that modulate consolidation strength and retrieval priority.
systems.description: Emotional coloring of memories that biases what is stored and recalled. Salience-based modulation is on the roadmap.

systems.name: Sensory Memory
systems.cognifold_mechanism: Planned: ultra-short multimodal buffers feeding raw perceptual traces into the event stream.
systems.description: Brief, high-fidelity retention of raw perceptual input before it decays or enters working memory. Multimodal sensory buffers are planned.

circuits.cognifold: EVENT layer — verbatim episodic capture; fast one-shot writes preserve what-happened-when.
CONCEPT layer — slow generalization; repeated EVENTs fold into grounded semantic structure.
INTENT layer + hierarchical context window — goals crystallize forward; the working set is assembled per event.
TIME anchors — deadlines and schedules indexed; recency and urgency reshape salience.
Inter-session consolidation — similar CONCEPTs merge and grounded structure is reinforced offline.
Forgetting — recency decay lowers stale salience; orphan pruning removes unsupported nodes.
Procedure nodes (planned) — reusable skills and action sequences, distinct from declarative recall.
Affective salience tags (planned) — emotional weighting that modulates consolidation and retrieval priority.
Perceptual buffers (planned) — ultra-short multimodal traces feed the event stream; priming via residual activation.
